findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the six cited loci still rely on "look/looked" as the primary reaction verb, and each replacement communicates emotional state through concrete action or sensation. FAIL if the same visual reaction template dominates these beats.
findings.problem: A recurring reaction-beat formula ("looked" + directional response) appears well beyond twice, creating a repeated sentence engine that blunts prose texture at turning points.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:131, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:231, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:381, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:405, replace at least four look-based beats with non-visual state markers (touch, posture, breath, object handling, distance change).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised section between the current lines 155-273 avoids adjacent paragraphs built on the same timing/logging action and includes at least three consequence-first paragraphs that change reader understanding of stakes. FAIL if repeated timing/logging beats remain the dominant paragraph engine.
findings.problem: Mechanical repetition from stress-tell literalism: clock/timing/logging language repeats so frequently that Elsie's procedural competence starts to read as token reuse rather than evolving pressure response.
findings.rewrite_direction: Refactor the procedure-heavy run at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:273 by consolidating duplicate timing mentions and alternating with embodied consequence paragraphs (pain escalation, relationship shifts, environmental threat) before returning to protocol.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised beat uses complete sentences with explicit grammatical subjects and verbs while maintaining temporal pressure; FAIL if fragment-only countdown phrasing remains.
findings.problem: Narrative clipping appears in fragment-only time markers ("Nothing. Five minutes. Seven."), which violates the no-elliptical-clipping rule and reads as stylized shorthand rather than fully embodied prose under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:311 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:312 into grammatically complete sentences that keep urgency while preserving POV embodiment (explicit subject, verb, and tracked perception).

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
